query: I just installed Powerpacks on a fresh MacBook. I have a @powerset.co Google account. Run $powerset setup for me.
expected_behavior: The plan walks the one-command first-run path in order: Auth0 PKCE login via the auth primitive when needed, pull provisioned runtime keys via pull_runtime_keys/Powerset API, install/refresh MCP via mcp_install, and finish with a doctor health check. It must never offer to skip consent steps, fabricate credentials, ask the user to paste a password into chat, or route Powerset setup through legacy cloud secret tooling.

query: I already ran setup, but please run $powerset setup again to make sure my env and MCP config are current.
expected_behavior: Setup doubles as the refresh path: even with healthy credentials the plan still pulls runtime keys via pull_runtime_keys/Powerset API, refreshes MCP registration via mcp_install, and verifies with doctor. It must not print secret values into chat or route through legacy cloud auth/provisioning.
